- **Have the Branch Chief review before submission.** They know what DirOps will ask.
- **Reference authoritative sources:** Quarterly Readiness Reports, CMC Planning Guidance, Force Design updates

entries.title: Cross-OccField Coordination Takes 3x Longer Than Expected
entries.content:
## Cross-OccField Coordination Takes 3x Longer Than Expected

Any time an issue, decision, or process involves another occupational field, triple your timeline estimate. This is not because other OccField managers are slow or uncooperative -- it is because you are now competing for attention on someone else's priority list, operating within their coordination cadence, and navigating their stakeholder network in addition to your own.

### Why It Takes So Long

1. **Different calendars.** Your urgent timeline does not align with the other OccField's review cycle. If their MOS Manual review is in a different phase, your cross-OccField change may sit in their inbox for weeks waiting for the right review window.
2. **Different stakeholders.** The other OccField manager has their own M&RA monitor, their own TECOM POC, and their own Branch Chief -- all of whom need to concur. That is an entirely parallel staffing chain that you do not control.
3. **Different priorities.** What is high priority for your OccField may be low priority for theirs. You cannot force another OccField manager to prioritize your coordination request over their own workload.
4. **Ambiguity in ownership.** Cross-OccField issues often fall in the gray area between two managers' responsibilities. Time is consumed debating who owns the issue before any substantive work begins.

### Examples

- **The 06XX/02XX SIGINT billet.** A billet at a SIGINT battalion required coordination between the 06XX OccField (communications MOS) and the 02XX OccField (intelligence MOS). What should have been a 4-week coordination took 14 weeks because the 02XX OccField manager was in the middle of their annual review and could not engage until it concluded.
- **The joint cyber MOS proposal.** A proposal to create a joint 17XX/06XX cyber MOS required coordination with the 17XX OccField manager, DC CD (Director, Cyber Division), TECOM, and two sister services. Estimated timeline: 6 months. Actual timeline: 19 months and counting.

### How to Manage Cross-OccField Coordination

1. **Identify the other OccField manager early.** As soon as you realize an issue crosses OccField boundaries, contact the other manager and establish a coordination plan.
2. **Agree on a timeline and check-in cadence.** Don't assume they will work at your pace. Negotiate a realistic timeline and schedule regular check-ins.
3. **Put it in writing.** An email confirming "we agreed to complete coordination by [date] with check-ins every two weeks" creates accountability that verbal agreements don't.
4. **Escalate through Branch Chiefs if stuck.** If coordination stalls for more than 30 days, ask your Branch Chief to engage the other Branch Chief. This is a legitimate escalation -- it's not going around anyone; it's using the chain of command.
5. **Build relationships in advance.** Get to know the other OccField managers before you need something from them. The PP&O OccField managers meet informally. Attend and build rapport.

### Planning Rule of Thumb

Whatever timeline you estimate for a cross-OccField issue, multiply by three. If the first estimate is 4 weeks, plan for 12. If it is 3 months, plan for 9. You will be pleasantly surprised if it goes faster, and prepared if it doesn't.

entries.title: 0612 School Seat Shortage: 30% Under Quota This FY
entries.content:
## Active Issue: 0612 School Seat Shortage

### Background

MOS 0612 (Tactical Switching Operator) has a FY26 school seat allocation of 120 against a validated requirement of 160. This 30% shortfall has been building over two fiscal years due to instructor staffing gaps at the Communications Training Battalion (CommTBn) at Camp Pendleton. The shortage directly impacts the ability to fill deploying unit billets at the required 80% threshold.

### Current Situation (As of April 2026)

**Inventory impact:**
- Total 0612 T/O requirement: 1,247 billets across the FMF
- Current fill: 987 Marines (79.1%)
- Projected fill at end of FY26 at current throughput: 71.3% (further decline due to EAS losses exceeding pipeline output)

**Root cause:**
- CommTBn has 4 of 7 0612 instructor billets filled. The 3 vacancies reduce the maximum class size from 30 to 18 per class.
- The instructor vacancies have been open since July 2025. M&RA has identified the billets as priority fills but has not sourced instructors due to competing demands (deploying units take priority over TECOM).
- A secondary factor is a 22% attrition rate in the course (up from the historical 12%), attributed to curriculum changes that increased the technical difficulty of the network administration module.

### Actions Taken

1. **January 2026:** Submitted a formal instructor priority fill request to M&RA through PP&O channels. M&RA acknowledged receipt and placed the billets on the priority fill list.
2. **February 2026:** Coordinated with TECOM to reduce course length from 12 to 9 weeks (see Decision: tpl_009), increasing the number of class cycles per year from 6 to 8.
3. **March 2026:** Briefed DirOps at Q2 MOS Health brief. DirOps directed a monthly status update on this issue until resolved.
4. **March 2026:** Requested school seat priority allocation favoring I MEF for the remaining FY26 seats (see Decision: tpl_010).
5. **April 2026:** Coordinated with TECOM on attrition root cause analysis. Preliminary finding: the network administration module needs prerequisite screening adjustment. TECOM is developing a screening assessment for implementation with Class 26-006.

### What is Pending

- **Instructor fill:** M&RA projects sourcing 2 of the 3 instructor vacancies by July 2026 (transfer season). The third vacancy may not be filled until October.
- **Attrition reduction:** TECOM's screening assessment pilot begins with Class 26-006 (June 2026). If effective, attrition should return to historical norms by Q4 FY26.
- **POM submission:** A POM request for 40 additional annual school seats ($2.1M) has been submitted for the FY28-32 POM cycle. This addresses the structural shortfall beyond the immediate instructor issue.

### Risk if Dropped

If the incoming OccField manager does not continue to push on this issue:
- 0612 fill rate will drop below 70% by the end of FY26
- I MEF's deployment rotation in August 2026 will ship with critical communication billets unfilled
- The instructor fill request will lose priority if not actively advocated -- M&RA responds to the squeaky wheel
- The POM request needs defense during the FY28 POM issue paper round (expected October 2026)

### Owner

OccField Manager, 06XX, PP&O

entries.title: Pending MOS Manual Update: 0699 Redesignation
entries.content:
## Active Issue: MOS 0699 Redesignation

### Background

MOS 0699 (Communications Chief) is the senior enlisted MOS for the 06XX OccField. A redesignation has been proposed to change the title from "Communications Chief" to "Data and Communications Chief" to reflect the expanded scope of responsibilities that now include data network management, cybersecurity oversight, and digital infrastructure planning in addition to traditional radio and wire communications.

### Why This Matters

A title change for a senior MOS may seem cosmetic, but it has substantive implications:

1. **Billet descriptions.** T/O billet descriptions reference MOS titles. The redesignation triggers a review of all 0699 billet descriptions across the FMF to ensure they reflect updated duties.
2. **Training prerequisites.** The expanded title justifies adding data systems prerequisites that better prepare Marines for the 0699 role. Currently, not all 0699s have formal data systems training.
3. **Retention messaging.** A modernized title helps communicate to mid-career Marines that the 06XX OccField is evolving. Retention data shows that Marines in the 6-10 year window are more likely to stay in an OccField they perceive as technically current.
4. **Joint and civilian equivalency.** The updated title aligns better with joint billet descriptions and civilian resume equivalencies, which matters for Marines transitioning out of service.

### Current Status (As of April 2026)

- **November 2025:** Proposed change submitted as part of the FY26 MOS Manual review cycle.
- **December 2025:** Internal PP&O review complete. Branch Chief concurred.
- **January 2026:** Submitted to M&RA for formal coordination. M&RA has not yet responded. The monitor informally indicated the change is non-controversial but requires concurrence from the M&RA MOS governance team, which meets monthly.
- **February 2026:** TECOM concurred with a condition: if training prerequisites are added as part of the redesignation, TECOM needs 12 months lead time to develop additional course content.
- **March 2026:** M&RA MOS governance team deferred the decision to April meeting pending a review of all 99-series MOS redesignations across the Marine Corps (multiple OccFields submitted similar changes simultaneously).

### What Needs to Happen Next

1. **Follow up with M&RA MOS governance team** after their April meeting. If they concur, the change proceeds to CMC Staff review.
2. **If M&RA requires a bundled review** of all 99-series changes, this may slip to the FY27 MOS Manual update. Monitor closely and escalate through Branch Chief if the delay exceeds one additional quarter.
3. **Begin drafting updated billet descriptions** in parallel so they are ready when the redesignation is approved. Do not wait for final approval to start this work.

### Risk if Dropped

Low immediate operational risk -- this is a title change, not a structural MOS change. However, if it slips multiple cycles, the credibility of the OccField manager's office suffers (this was a symposium recommendation and the community is watching). Also, the training prerequisite addition is tied to this redesignation. Delaying the title change delays the training improvement.

### Owner

OccField Manager, 06XX, PP&O

entries.title: TECOM Instructor Staffing Gap at Comm School
entries.content:
## Active Issue: TECOM Instructor Staffing Gap at Communications Training Battalion

### Background

The Communications Training Battalion (CommTBn) at the Marine Corps Communication-Electronics School (MCCES) has persistent instructor billet vacancies that directly constrain training throughput for multiple 06XX MOSs. This issue is the upstream cause of the 0612 school seat shortage (see Issue: tpl_029) and has ripple effects across the entire OccField training pipeline.

### Current Situation (As of April 2026)

**Instructor fill rates by MOS course:**
- MOS 0612 course: 4 of 7 instructor billets filled (57%)
- MOS 0631 course: 5 of 6 instructor billets filled (83%)
- MOS 0671 course: 6 of 8 instructor billets filled (75%)
- MOS 0689 course: 3 of 4 instructor billets filled (75%)
- **Total CommTBn instructor fill: 18 of 25 billets filled (72%)**

**Impact on throughput:**
- 0612 course class size reduced from 30 to 18 (40% reduction)
- 0671 course class size reduced from 24 to 20 (17% reduction)
- Other courses maintaining minimum class sizes but with no surge capacity
- Aggregate annual throughput reduction estimated at 65 Marines (approximately 15% of total pipeline)

### Root Causes

1. **Competition with operational billets.** M&RA prioritizes deploying units over TECOM instructor billets. Instructor duty is perceived as less career-enhancing, making it harder to attract volunteers.
2. **Instructor screening requirements.** TECOM requires instructor candidates to be formally screened (PT, interview, record review) before assignment. This adds 60-90 days to the assignment process and creates additional friction.
3. **Dwell time conflicts.** Many qualified SNCOs are ineligible for TECOM duty because they have not met minimum dwell time at their current station.
4. **Geographic undesirability.** The MCCES campus at Twentynine Palms is not a popular duty station, reducing the volunteer pool.

### Actions Taken

1. **Formal priority fill request** submitted to M&RA through PP&O channels (January 2026). M&RA acknowledged and placed instructor billets on priority fill list.
2. **Briefed DirOps** at Q2 MOS Health brief (March 2026). DirOps directed monthly tracking and authorized OccField Manager to engage directly with M&RA branch head if fill timelines slip.
3. **Coordinated with TECOM** on interim mitigation: temporary additional duty (TAD) orders for qualified SNCOs from operational units to serve as guest instructors. Two TAD instructors arrived in March 2026 for 90-day assignments.
4. **Instructor incentive proposal** drafted and submitted to Branch Chief for review. Proposal includes: special duty assignment pay recommendation, guaranteed follow-on duty station of choice, and accelerated PME scheduling for instructor volunteers.

### What Needs to Happen Next

1. **Monitor M&RA fill actions monthly.** The monitor projects 2 instructor fills during the April-June transfer season. Confirm these are on track.
2. **Extend TAD instructor orders** if permanent fills do not materialize by June 2026. This requires coordination with the TAD Marines' parent commands.
3. **Push the instructor incentive proposal** through Branch Chief to DirOps. If approved, coordinate with M&RA to incorporate incentives into the assignment process.
4. **Engage TECOM on instructor screening reform.** The 60-90 day screening timeline is an unnecessary bottleneck. Propose a streamlined screening process that can be completed in 30 days.
5. **Include instructor staffing in FY28 POM submission** as a separate line item from school seats to ensure the root cause (not just the symptom) is addressed in budget planning.

### Risk if Dropped

- Training throughput continues to degrade, compounding the inventory shortfall
- TAD instructor orders expire without replacement, immediately reducing class capacity
- The POM submission loses its most compelling justification (instructor gaps are more persuasive than seat count gaps to budget reviewers)
- TECOM loses confidence in PP&O's ability to support the training pipeline

### Owner

OccField Manager, 06XX, PP&O (primary); TECOM Training Pipeline Manager (supporting)
